Ad Text:
Pricing is the heartbeat of wagering. Lead the data science team that sets sharper prices across sport and racing, powering real-time trading decisions that directly shape margin and customer experience. Grow a high-performing team in a collaborative, high-trust culture that values curiosity, impact and fun.

How you'll be a part of the action 

Reporting to the Head of Wagering Data Analytics and Segmentation, you will lead the design, delivery and continuous improvement of pricing and risk models that drive performance across our Wagering Trading team. You will partner with traders, product and technology to turn ideas into measurable commercial results.

Lead development of probabilistic pricing models for sport and racing markets — from fixed-odds and exotic pricing to in-play and derivative markets.
Design and improve models that generate accurate and competitive prices across thoroughbred, harness, greyhound and multi-sport events.
Build models that account for market dynamics, form, conditions, venue and real-time signals to produce prices that balance competitiveness with margin.
Partner with traders to develop automated pricing and risk frameworks that reduce manual intervention and improve speed to market.
Create model monitoring and feedback loops — tracking price accuracy, overrounds, market movement and settlement outcomes to continuously improve model performance.
Shape and grow a high-performing data science team with clear standards, strong coaching and a focus on delivery.
Champion ethical, purpose-led use of data to enhance customer outcomes and meet regulatory obligations.

What you'll need to succeed

You are a curious, hands-on leader who balances scientific rigour with practical delivery. You understand how a wagering trading operation works and know what good pricing looks like — not just mathematically, but commercially.

Deep experience in wagering, sports betting or racing pricing — you understand how odds are formed, how markets move, and where model-driven pricing creates edge.
Proven track record building probabilistic models for event outcomes — win/place, head-to-head, line, total, multi-leg and in-play markets.
Experience working with racing form data, ratings, speed maps, track/weather conditions and translating these into pricing inputs.
Understanding of overround management, liability and risk exposure and how pricing models interact with trading decisions.
Familiarity with real-time data feeds and low-latency environments — pricing models that need to perform under event-time pressure, not just in batch.
Proven ability to lead, coach and grow data scientists and modellers.
Strong programming capability in Python and SQL, with experience in modern ML frameworks and cloud platforms (Databricks, AWS, MLflow or similar).
Ability to translate complex analysis into clear stories and decisions for traders, product teams and senior leaders.
Proven capability to design model governance, documentation and testing disciplines.

What will set you apart

Experience with Australian racing and/or sport wagering markets specifically.
Background in Bayesian modelling, simulation or stochastic methods applied to pricing.
Exposure to reinforcement learning or dynamic pricing optimisation for in-play or live markets.
Understanding of customer-level pricing dynamics — how price sensitivity, segmentation and market positioning interact with margin targets.
Experience operating within a licensed, regulated wagering environment and understanding of responsible gambling obligations.

What's in it for you

We know that when we belong, we thrive. That’s why we offer:

Time for moments that matter - from birthdays to cultural leave, flexible public holidays, and 5 days bonus leave
18 weeks paid parental leave for every new parent, no matter your role
Ticketing to some of the country’s biggest racing and sports events.
Access to our leading wellbeing app and programs to help you be your best
A range of corporate discounts and special offers with leading retailers
A diverse and inclusive culture where everyone belongs

Get to know us

We’re Tabcorp - home to TAB, Sky Racing, and MAX, shaping the future of our industry through digital innovation and cutting-edge technology. With a strong retail presence and a growing digital footprint, we connect customers wherever they play. We’re innovators and big thinkers in a fast-paced, exciting sector, committed to safer gambling and responsible play. Here, you’ll belong where the action is and make Tabcorp your home ground.

Join the action

Ready to make Tabcorp your home ground? Join a team that thrives on collaboration, inclusion, respect, and face to face connection. We welcome and support people of all abilities - if you need adjustments during the process, email car••••@tabcorp.com.au.

Commercial Finance Business Partner | Global Manufacturer | South West Sydney | Hybrid | Up to $150K +Bonus + Super

About the Company
A well-established, globally connected manufacturing business with a strong local presence in South West Sydney. This is a business with serious growth ambitions, targeting more than a doubling of revenue over the next five years through m&a activity, organic growth and expansion into new markets. The finance function is central to making that happen, and this role sits right at the heart of it.

The Role
This is not a score keeping role. Reporting to the Head of Finance, this is a genuinely commercial position sitting at the intersection of finance, strategy and operations. You will be the dedicated finance partner to the Commercial Director and the broader Sales and Marketing teams, with a mandate to challenge, govern and drive better commercial decision-making as the business scales aggressively.

If the idea of being in the room where growth strategy is stress-tested, supported and executed appeals to you, read on.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Partnering with Sales & Marketing to understand what is driving performance and translating that into clear, actionable financial insight
Building scenario models and assessing the commercial viability of growth initiatives, protecting gross margin through a period of significant expansion
Leading the commercial component of monthly Demand Review and S&OP processes, owning commercial inputs into group forecast & budget submissions
Driving automation and self-service reporting through Power BI and emerging automation tools, freeing the team up for higher-value work
What They Are Looking For
This role suits someone who is as comfortable on the floor talking to a sales & operations leaders as they are building a scenario model. You are commercially curious, analytically sharp, and you have the professional backbone to push back and challenge the status quo. Critically, you thrive in environments where the pace is fast and the stakes are real.

In addition to the above the successful candidate will be someone who:

Demonstrates advanced financial modelling skills, including ROI and volume break-even
Has a strong grasp of P&L drivers and gross-to-net margin mechanics
Proficient in Power BI, with an appetite for automation and emerging technology
Views AI and emerging technology as a strategic tool
A no-surprises communicator who brings solutions, not just problems to stakeholders
Thrives in a fast-paced environment where the pace of change is real
Qualifications and Experience

CA or CPA qualified is ideal, though strong commercial acumen will be weighted heavily
Manufacturing, FMCG, Pharma, Retail, Logistics industry backgrounds will be highly regarded
Salary and Benefits
Up to $150,000 + bonus + superannuation.
A genuinely flexible work environment where life commitments outside the office are supported and respected. Hybrid working is part of the deal. The office is based in South West Sydney.

Ad Text:

Box Hill Institute is entering a significant period of growth and transformation, with major investment underway across our IT and AI implementation space. APPLY NOW and be part of what's next.

Translate business needs into clear, traceable and testable requirements, supported by impact and feasibility analysis

Knowledge of, or certification in, project and delivery methodologies (Agile, PRINCE2, Scrum or similar)

Are you ready to shape the future of data architecture and help build a modern, scalable, and insights-driven enterprise?

We’re seeking an experienced Data Architect to lead the design and delivery of next-generation data solutions — from data lakes and integration frameworks to advanced analytics platforms.

As the Data Architect, you’ll play a key role in defining and delivering the organisation’s enterprise data architecture. In addition, you’ll ensure data is structured, governed, and leveraged effectively to support operational and strategic decision-making.

You’ll provide architectural leadership across integration, data warehousing, analytics, and governance while fostering collaboration between business units, technical teams, and senior leadership.

Key Responsibilities of the Data Architect will include:

Develop and maintain enterprise-wide data architecture strategies, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with business goals.
Lead solution design for data platforms, integrations, data lakes, and BI/reporting systems using modern architectures (batch, streaming, APIs, microservices).
Create conceptual, logical, and physical data models across structured and unstructured data using best-practice frameworks (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault).
Define and enforce data standards for quality, metadata, security, privacy, and compliance.
Architect data solutions across Azure, Snowflake, Informatica, and hybrid/multi-cloud environments.
Collaborate with stakeholders and technical teams to translate business needs into data-driven solutions.
Guide and mentor engineering teams on CI/CD, automation, and modern data engineering practices.
Optimise data systems for performance, scalability, and reliability.
Promote and embed a strong data culture across the organisation.
Skills & Experience required to measure success:

5 + years’ experience in data architecture, solution design, and enterprise data management.
Proven success architecting large-scale BI, analytics, and integration platforms.
Strong knowledge of cloud and hybrid ecosystems (Azure, AWS, Snowflake, Informatica, Databricks).
Expertise in data modelling (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault 2.0).
Experience with data engineering tools, CI/CD, and DevOps practices.
Deep understanding of governance, metadata, and master data management.
Experience with modern integration approaches (APIs, event-driven, streaming).
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and leadership capability

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field (required).
Postgraduate qualification in Data Science or Information Systems (highly regarded).
Certifications in Microsoft, Azure, Snowflake, or Informatica (desirable).
Apply now if you’re passionate about leading enterprise-scale data architecture and building data ecosystems that enable real business impact!

We are more than developers or builders - we are visionaries, and our ability to see the world differently drives us to be bold, embrace innovation and diversity, and lead with optimism. Your Opportunity

You will consolidate financial and operational results, lead business improvement initiatives, and build financial models end-to-end.

You will also support budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis - providing insights that inform decision‑making and help senior management drive performance and growth.

Coordinate and support financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting cycles for divisions and portfolios, ensuring alignment with Development and Group strategy.
Maintain and interpret development project budgets and forecasts, partnering with Commercial Finance to review assumptions, ensure accounting outcomes, and report at both project and portfolio levels using Anaplan and Excel-based models.

Your Point of Difference

To be successful, you will bring:

This is an ideal role for an FP&A Analyst with 6-7 years' experience in chartered accounting and commercial environments looking to step up into a Senior role.

You will bring:


CPA/CA qualification (essential)
Experience with business tools/systems such as Microsoft BI, Tableau, Salesforce, and Anaplan
Property industry exposure (desirable)
Strong stakeholder engagement and problem‑solving skills are essential
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Well‑developed analytical and problem‑solving skills
Strong technical accounting and financial modelling capability
Advanced Excel skills
Excellent attention to detail
Demonstrated ability to lead, motivate, and mentor finance team members
Passion, a self‑motivated can‑do attitude, and the ability to work to deadlines

Working at Mirvac

At Mirvac, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We foster a workplace built on trust, collaboration, inclusivity, and continuous learning. You'll join a supportive leader and an experienced team, with exposure across Commercial, Residential, and cross‑functional groups.

If you're an ambitious accounting professional ready to take the next step into FP&A within one of Australia's most respected property groups, we'd love to hear from you.

Ad Text:
Insights and Analytics Unit, Sunshine Coast or Moreton Bay Campus
Level 10, $148,551 p.a., plus up to 17% employer superannuation
Full-time, ongoing appointment

The Opportunity

Data and information are central to UniSC’s strategy, performance and impact. The Insights and Analytics Unit plays a central role in enabling the University to use data and insights to inform decision-making, support sustainable growth, and strengthen outcomes for students, staff and the broader community.

Reporting to the Chief Data Officer, the Senior Lead, Data Analytics is a senior leadership role within the Insights and Analytics Unit (IAU), responsible for delivering UniSC’s enterprise data analytics roadmap while providing hands-on leadership to modernise analytics capabilities, uplift the University’s enterprise data warehouse and reporting environments, and strengthen data products and ways of working across the University.

You will operate in a highly collaborative, distributed environment, working closely with executive leaders, academic and professional teams, and technology specialists to embed an insight-driven culture and ensure data is leveraged responsibly, effectively and at scale.

You will

lead the delivery of UniSC’s enterprise data analytics roadmap, translating strategy into a prioritised, value‑driven program of work;
oversee the end-to-end design and delivery of modern analytics solutions, including scalable enterprise data platforms and integrated reporting, from data ingestion through to dashboards, reports and advanced insights;
partner with executive and senior stakeholders to embed an insight‑driven culture and deliver analytics that support key strategic and operational decisions;
lead and develop specialist analytics teams, while championing strong data governance, privacy and information management practices.

About you

You are a seasoned data and analytics leader who thrives in complex environments and enjoys translating strategy into action. You bring a balance of technical depth, program leadership and stakeholder influence, with a strong commitment to delivering analytics that genuinely improve outcomes. You bring experience delivering contemporary analytics ecosystems and enterprise data environments that enable scalable, trusted and accessible insights across complex organisations. You are comfortable operating at executive level, leading specialist teams, and navigating competing priorities across a large, distributed organisation. Just as importantly, you value collaboration, inclusivity and building capability in others.

You will possess:

Extensive experience with modern data analytics and business intelligence technologies, particularly within the Microsoft analytics ecosystem.
Proven capability leading large, complex, technology-enabled programs or portfolios of work.
Strong expertise in designing data warehouses and delivering high-quality dashboards, reports and analytics products that meet real user needs.
Highly developed communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence, negotiate and lead through collaboration.
Experience applying contemporary analytics and data practices in complex organisations, ideally within higher education or similarly nuanced environments.
A proactive, innovative approach to problem-solving and leading change at scale.

Management Consultant (Senior Associate/Manager/Senior Manager) – Expression of Interest

Melbourne or Sydney | #LI-Hybrid

Reach your remarkable at Grant Thornton

Grant Thornton Australia is one of the world’s leading independently owned and managed accounting, advisory and consulting firms. Our culture is underpinned by a commitment to our clients, our people and our communities, and our promise to Reach for Remarkable—delivering proven expertise, principled execution and a personalised experience.

In Australia, we proudly support the careers of over 1,500 remarkable people across six offices nationwide, with partner firms in more than 145 markets globally. Wherever you want to go, we can take you there.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Associate, Manager or Senior Manager in our Management Consulting team, you will provide strategic and operational advice to organisations, supporting them to deliver measurable performance improvement in a timely and efficient manner.

You will work closely with senior client stakeholders to identify needs, shape solutions aligned to strategy and business requirements, and lead delivery teams to successful outcomes. This role combines hands-on project leadership, trusted advisory, and people leadership, with opportunities to contribute to the growth and capability of our Management Consulting practice.

How you will make an impact

Understand clients’ businesses and apply management consulting expertise to diagnose issues and identify performance improvement opportunities
Build strong, trusted relationships with clients by actively engaging, providing guidance and delivering practical outcomes
Evaluate and analyse client financial performance, processes, systems and organisational structures
Design clear, structured initiatives aligned to clients’ strategic direction and business requirements
Develop and present recommendations and proposals to improve methods, processes and operating models
Lead the implementation and delivery of approved recommendations
Review and quality-check findings and recommendations within the context of the client’s broader business environment
Manage multiple engagements concurrently, ensuring timelines, quality and stakeholder expectations are met
Lead, coach and develop engagement teams, fostering capability and high performance
Contribute to continuous improvement of quality, methodologies and best practice within the team

About you and what you'll bring

You are a seasoned consulting professional who brings clarity to complex problems and momentum to delivery. Known for your structured thinking and practical approach, you balance commercial insight with a strong focus on outcomes. You are comfortable operating in ambiguous environments, can translate strategy into actionable change, and are trusted by senior stakeholders to lead complex engagements through to measurable results. Just as importantly, you bring others with you, building capability, confidence and performance across teams.

Proven experience at Senior Associate, Manager or Senior Manager level in a management consulting or professional services environment
Demonstrated advisory, project management and client-facing experience delivering complex engagements
Strong business and operational acumen, with the ability to analyse performance and identify improvement opportunities
Experience in change management, transformation and/or organisational development
A strong track record of managing senior stakeholders and leading diverse, high‑performing project teams
Strong analytical, critical thinking and problem-solving capability
Commercial insight, with experience contributing to proposals or business development initiatives
Highly developed communication skills, including executive-level written and verbal presentation
Demonstrated people leadership capability, including coaching and mentoring consultants
Tertiary qualification in a relevant discipline; MBA or postgraduate qualifications advantageous
If you’re interested in this role but don’t feel you meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply and share the unique skills and perspectives you can bring to our team.

Why Grant Thornton?

At Grant Thornton, we do things differently. We know that when you strive for better and care about what you do, remarkable things are possible. You’ll be supported by passionate leaders and collaborative teams, empowered to shape a career that’s tailored to you, and encouraged to make a meaningful impact for clients and communities.

Remarkable perks that put you first:

Flexible working options to support balance and wellbeing
Free annual gym membership
Active employee networks and social committees
Family-focused support, including 26 weeks paid parental leave and superannuation during parental leave
Mental health and wellbeing tools, resources and coaching
And many more benefits designed to support you to thrive

#LI-Hybrid

Company overview

Alloggio Group is Australia's leading provider of professionally managed short-term rental accommodation.

With a growing portfolio of over 30 localised brands across Australia and New Zealand, we are dedicated to delivering exceptional guest experiences and high-performing results for property owners. Our central team in Brisbane supports a rapidly scaling network of regional operations .

Role overview

We are seeking a highly motivated and commercially minded finance professional to join our dynamic finance team in Brisbane South Bank. This role will play a key part in supporting strategic decision-making through high quality financial analysis, forecasting, modelling and business partnering across the Group.

The role will be heavily involved in budgeting, forecasting, cash flow management, Board and bank reporting, operational performance analysis and strategic initiatives. The successful candidate will also provide commercial oversight across data and BI reporting, helping ensure reporting integrity, consistency and actionable insights across the business. Additionally, the role will support mergers and acquisitions activity, including due diligence, investment analysis and integration planning.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A)

• Support the preparation of weekly, monthly, quarterly and annual forecasts (Cash Flow and P&L), including annual budgets.
• Prepare quarterly covenant reporting and supporting packs for banking partners.
• Perform monthly and ad hoc variance analysis and provide clear insights into key business drivers and financial performance.
• Prepare executive-level financial reporting and presentations for senior leadership, Board reporting and stakeholders.
• Develop and maintain financial models to support forecasting, scenario planning, profitability analysis and strategic decision-making.
• Support working capital, cash flow and operational performance analysis across the Group.

Commercial Finance & Business Partnering

• Partner with operational and commercial leaders to support pricing, margin improvement and cost optimisation initiatives.
• Provide commercial insights and financial analysis to support strategic initiatives, contract negotiations and operational decision-making.
• Work cross-functionally with business leaders to identify growth opportunities and improve overall financial performance.
• Support preparation of monthly Board packs and ad hoc executive reporting.
• Undertake ad hoc modelling and analysis depending on business requirements.

Data & BI Oversight

• Provide commercial oversight of business reporting, dashboards and BI outputs.
• Work closely with offshore Data & BI Engineers, operational teams and business leaders to ensure the business is receiving accurate, timely and commercially meaningful insights.
• Improve reporting integrity, automation and consistency across the business.
• Assist in identifying opportunities to enhance reporting capability, operational insights and decision support tools.
• Ensure financial and operational reporting aligns with business objectives and executive reporting requirements.

Mergers & Acquisitions

• Lead and support financial due diligence on potential acquisitions across Holidays and Mixed Accommodation businesses.
• Engage with prospective sellers and external advisors throughout acquisition processes.
• Prepare investment papers and financial analysis for Board review, including acquisition recommendations and financing considerations.
• Support acquisition integration planning and post-acquisition performance tracking.

Financial Reporting & Compliance

• Prepare and review monthly financial reports, ensuring accuracy and consistency with accounting records.
• Support improvements to reporting processes, controls and financial governance.

• Assist with internal and external audits and ensure financial information remains accurate and up to date.

Who we're looking for:

We’re looking for a commercially minded and analytical finance professional with strong experience in financial analysis, budgeting, forecasting, and commercial performance reporting. The ideal candidate is data-driven, detail-oriented, and confident partnering with stakeholders to support strategic and operational decision-making across the business.

• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics or a related field.
• CPA, CA, CFA or CIMA qualifications preferred.
• 5+ years’ experience in commercial finance, FP&A, corporate finance or similar analytical finance roles.
• Strong financial modelling, forecasting and budgeting experience.
• Proven business partnering capability with the ability to communicate financial information clearly to non-financial stakeholders.
• Experience supporting senior leadership and strategic decision-making.
• Strong Excel, analytical and problem-solving skills.
• Excellent communication and presentation skills.
• Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
• Highly organised, commercially focused and proactive in approach.

Why join us?

The career growth opportunities at Alloggio are limitless. With ambitious goals to triple in size over the next three to five years, you'll get the opportunity to be a part of a dynamic team achieving things that've never been done in ANZ before.

On top of career growth, you'll benefit from:

Flexibility — this role offer some flexibity to work from home

A competitive market salary

The opportunity to work with household names in the tourism sector

Automony to take control of your day-to-day

A Brisbane-based team with technical, and creative support on standby

Staff discounts

We are seeking a hands-on Data Platform Lead to own and evolve a modern cloud-native data environment. You will lead the design and delivery of scalable data engineering solutions, enabling real-time data processing, advanced analytics, AI/ML capability, and self-service reporting across the organisation.

This role will focus on driving innovation through modern data platforms, Generative AI, and automation while establishing robust data architecture, pipelines, governance, and engineering best practices.

You and your team will be responsible for bringing together structured and unstructured data from multiple sources and transforming it into meaningful insights for advanced analytics and AI initiatives.

You will also play a key role in establishing and growing cutting-edge AI capabilities as the organisation continues its journey towards embedding AI into its services and operations.

What You'll Bring Benfits
Strong experience across data engineering, data architecture, and cloud-native platforms (Azure preferred)
Exposure to Fabric, Databricks, and Snowflake highly desirable
Proven experience leading enterprise data platform initiatives
Expertise in data modelling, ETL/ELT pipelines, data warehousing, and real-time processing
Exposure to AI/ML platforms, Generative AI, and MLOps practices
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Passion for innovation, continuous improvement, and modern data capability uplift

This is an exciting opportunity for someone who considers themselves an experienced, hands-on leader within the data space and is looking for their next challenge.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer - Finance Data Platform
Ad Text:
Play a key role in supporting the Australian economy.
Discounted food, travel, entertainment, gym membership, staff parking and, + more.
Hybrid working environment.

Company

For 81 years, we've achieved success through our ability to innovate, placing a clear emphasis on how we best serve our customers’ needs. As demand continues to grow, we have expanded rapidly, now delivering a superior range of dry goods, frozen and chilled products, fresh seafood and meat, confectionery, paper products, and cleaning solutions.

In recent years, we’ve seen significant growth and invested in our systems and processes, building a close partnership with Woolworths Group that has continued to deliver high-quality service for our customers and suppliers.

In 2024, Woolworths moved to a 100% interest in the PFD business, bringing PFD exciting opportunities as a strategic part of the Group. Sharing expertise across both teams will ensure our continued success and drive further growth and unlock our collective potential.

About this Position  

We’re seeking a Senior Data Engineer (Financial Systems & Analytics) to own and evolve our finance data platform, supporting critical planning, budgeting, and forecasting capabilities across the business. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering and finance, responsible for building robust data pipelines and architecture while partnering closely with finance stakeholders to ensure data is accurate, reliable, and fit for purpose. You will take ownership of our current financial planning environment (including TM1) while playing a key role in shaping and supporting future system enhancements and transformation initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Design and maintain scalable data pipelines integrating ERP, Financial Planning Systems (Anaplan, Onestream, Workday, and others), and other business systems
Build and optimise data models to support financial reporting, planning, and forecasting (e.g. P&L, balance sheet)
Manage and enhance the financial data platform (Databricks/Lakehouse), ensuring performance, reliability, and scalability
Support and maintain financial systems (including TM1), ensuring continuity of business-critical processes
Partner with finance stakeholders to translate reporting and planning requirements into data solutions
Contribute to system improvements and future state design, including potential financial planning tool migration
Ensure strong data governance, quality, and security controls across financial datasets

What We’re Looking For

To thrive in this role, you will bring:

Strong understanding of financial planning and accounting concepts essential (e.g. P&L, forecasting, budgeting cycles)
Proven experience as a Senior Data Engineer, building and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines and data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Spark, SQL)
Experience working with financial data or finance systems (e.g. TM1 or similar EPM tools)
Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and dimensional modelling (star/snowflake schemas)
Ability to partner with finance stakeholders and translate business requirements into scalable data solutions
Experience supporting and maintaining production systems in a business-critical environment
Exposure to system implementation or migration projects (highly regarded)

What PFD is Offering the Successful Candidate

We are offering competitive remuneration, superannuation and other staff benefits (such as discounts on staff purchases, discounted accommodation, health insurance and banking).

Culture & Values

Customer Focus: Know your external and internal customers. Gain and preserve their confidence because we can only be successful if our customers are successful.
Respect: Treat people at work and in the community as they would like to be treated. Act safely, responsibly, and ethically. 
Passion: Share the passion of making us a successful and dynamic company. Let’s celebrate successes.
Simplicity: Let’s minimise the complications of doing business so customer services are on time, accurate and reliable. Share ideas to improve our company.
Leadership: All of us can be leaders in our day to day working lives. Bring out the best in yourself and the people around you. Do what you say you are going to do.

How to Apply
If this sounds like your kind of challenge, we’d love to hear from you! Click the apply button and submit your updated resume and a cover letter.

Applications close Monday, 1 June 2026 at 12:00 pm (AEST). Early applications are encouraged, as hiring managers may begin shortlisting candidates before the closing date. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be treated confidentially.

As part of our recruitment process, do you consent to undergoing employment checks? These may include a medical assessment (which may involve drug and alcohol testing), a National Police History Check, a Working with Children/Vulnerable Groups Check, and a Licence Validation/Demerit Point Check.

Please note: We do not accept unsolicited CVs from recruitment agencies or third parties. Any CVs sent directly to Line Managers will not be considered, and we will not be responsible for any associated fees or costs.

TRANSFORMATION AGILE COACH - ALLIANZ TECHNOLOGY - NORTH SYDNEY

With over 13,000 employees across 20+ countries, Allianz Technology is the go-to technology partner for Allianz Group companies across the world. We're all about crafting world-class, agile, and sustainable IT solutions that power a seamless digital journey for our customers. From massive IT infrastructure projects like data centres and networks to innovative application platforms in workplace services, data science, and customer interfaces, we're at the forefront of building the future.

We're proud to be a global pioneer of change, as single centre of excellence, acting as Allianz's digital backbone worldwide. With our streamlined approach, we simplify Allianz's business operations wherever they are, by delivering full-scale, end-to-end IT solutions for Allianz in the digital age. 

Let’s care for tomorrow, so we can create a better future together, for everyone.

 About the role

The primary purpose of this role is to lead the cultural and process Agile transformation within the organization.  The Role will be responsible for operationalising the Agile Blueprint to establish and maintain agile structures, define and align Agile roles, and implement Agile processes to ensure the successful execution of delivery.  This role is pivotal in driving the shift towards a lean, cloud-based enterprise, fostering a culture of autonomy, and optimizing the IT value stream for speed and safety.

·       Lead the Agile transformation by leveraging the Agile Blueprint as a guide for the Allianz Technology’s Agile journey. Ensuring that all delivery pillars are following the blue print and any exceptions in process, tools and roles are documented.

·       Set up and maintain agile structures that support the development of high-performing teams and the organization's strategic goals.

·       Lead on the cultural journey to an Agile mind set organisation from roles, responsibilities, structures, capabilities to behaviours.

·       Collaborate with stakeholders to establish and align Agile roles according to the Agile Blueprint, ensuring clarity of responsibilities and efficient team dynamics.

·       Establish and refine Agile processes and tools that contribute to the success of delivery, including the systematic elimination of waste and the optimization of workflows.

·       Establish the Governance Structures process, connected to the demand management of Allianz Australia, aligned to the Group’s Agile Framework.

·       Be an ambassador for Agile and the Agile Blueprint, within and outside the team, by providing advice to leaders, managers and delivery teams to support company, team and individual performance development.

·       Champion technical excellence and the continuous improvement of processes, tools, and techniques to drive innovation and efficiency.

·       Support and encourage the application transformation from monolithic architectures to modern microservices, and infrastructure-as-code programmatically built into fundamentals of across various cloud providers technologies effectively.

·       Facilitate the organization-wide implementation of DevOps, rethinking organizational structures, IT governance, and ITSM practices to enhance speed and safety.

·       Provide organisation wide training and ongoing guidance for the use of Agile methodologies, such as SCRUM and KANBAN, across multiple development locations and time zones building out target products.

 

About you

·       Proven experience in leading Agile transformations in large, complex organizations.

·       Strong understanding of Agile methodologies, frameworks, and best practices.

·       Experience with infrastructure-as-code and cloud technologies.

·       Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.

·       Ability to collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders across the organization.

·       Knowledge of DevOps practices and principles.

·       Experience in application and organizational transformation towards lean and agile operations, establishing governance, processes, and organisation change.

 

About our culture

We care about everything that makes you, you. We believe in a workplace that celebrates inclusion and equal opportunity, where people of all genders, ages, religions, sexual orientations and abilities are not only welcomed but valued for the unique perspectives and talents they bring to work.  We’re committed to fostering an environment where everyone belongs and can thrive and reach their fullest potential.

 

Adjustments and support

If you require any support and adjustments to participate equitably in our recruitment process, we encourage you to reach out to car••••@allianz.com.au for a confidential conversation.

Randstad is partnering with a Federal Government Agency to recruit an EL1 Assistant Director - Strategic Pricing position. This role is critical in delivering robust economic research and pricing policy to ensure the sustainability of the human services market.

Assignment Overview

Pay Rate: $602.55 per day (excluding super).
Location: Flexible / National. Candidates can be based in Tasmania, with access to offices nationally and flexible hybrid working arrangements, Full-Time

Role Responsibilities

Working within the Strategic Pricing Advisory team, you will lead work to deliver evidence-based decision-making on complex economic and pricing policies. Your day-to-day will involve:

Leading Projects: Managing and contributing to the Annual Pricing Review and leading discrete pricing and consultation projects.
Economic Modelling: Applying statistical methods and research to large datasets to understand market functions and propose improvements.
Stakeholder Engagement: Building strong relationships with technical and non-technical stakeholders, experts, and academics.
Policy Development: Developing robust pricing frameworks and providing strategic advice to senior leadership regarding the "care economy".
Strategic Impact: Influencing the allocation of Scheme resources projected to grow significantly over the coming years.

Key Selection Criteria

The ideal candidate will possess a "quantitative mindset" and the ability to solve qualitative problems within a changing legislative environment.

Analytical Skills: Superior problem-solving skills applied to complex pricing and economic issues.
Technical Proficiency: Ability to handle and analyse data, with technical skills to communicate findings to diverse audiences.
Strategic Thinking: Experience in the development of pricing policies and analytical frameworks.
Collaboration: Proven ability to work well within a team while maintaining the ability to work independently.
Education/Experience: A bachelor's degree or higher in Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, or Public Policy is highly desirable.

Eligibility & Security

Citizenship: You must be an Australian Citizen to be eligible for this role.
Clearances: Successful candidates must undergo a National Criminal History Records Check and possess any clearances required to undertake the duties of the assignment.

How to Apply

To apply, please submit your CV with a brief cover letter.



At Randstad, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

About Sestra People

Sestra People is a boutique, female-founded talent provider dedicated to rewriting the narrative around recruitment. At Sestra People, we believe your next best hire isn’t just a CV—it’s a human being with quirks, flaws, talent, and a story.

We champion values of Kindness, Transparency, and Professional Results, without compromising on a family-feel, bringing back a sense of ‘humanness’ to recruitment.




The Opportunity

Sestra People have partnered with a boutique, premier consulting firm to find the best Data Engineers in Sydney. Not people waiting for tickets to be assigned. Not engineers hiding behind delivery managers. Not consultants who've spent years in giant bodyshops without ever truly owning a client relationship. Small team. High calibre.

This is for Senior to Principal-level consultants who can genuinely operate at the intersection of technical excellence, client partnership, critical thinking, and curiosity.

You'll work across the full lifecycle of data projects — architecture, modelling, ingestion, transformation, and delivery — with real client interaction on every engagement.

The bar is high for a reason. This isn't a seat-filler role.




Experience Required

Snowflake (preferred) or Databricks, Fabric or similar platforms

AWS or GCP (preferred) or Azure

Beyond the shiny tooling, I want people who have done the dirty work in data. People who've been around the block. Engineers with depth, scars, judgement, and strong fundamentals.

Designed databases from the ground up, with strong data modelling capability and deep data fundamentals

Built robust schemas, data models, and warehouse architecture from scratch

Worked deeply with performance, optimisation, governance, lineage, and architecture

Strong coding skills in Python, SQL, and Spark — fluency, not AI-assisted copy-paste

Spent years understanding how data systems actually work under the hood — not just abstraction layers and drag-and-drop tooling

Exceptional stakeholder management — able to lead a conversation with a CTO confidently, with an almost 'sales' capable way. Translating technical requirements to non technical audiences.

Strong consulting experience. You've sat in front of clients (not internal stakeholders), at C level and solved problems with zero hand holding. Ability to uncover problems, challenge thinking, and drive outcomes independently and a must have.




What You'll Do

Design and build scalable data pipelines and cloud-based data platforms in Snowflake

Work across the full lifecycle of data projects including architecture, modelling, ingestion, transformation, and delivery.

Apply strong data engineering fundamentals including schema design, dimensional modelling, and warehouse architecture.

Collaborate closely with clients to understand business requirements and translate them into robust technical solutions.

Design systems that are resilient, maintainable, and scalable, following best-practice engineering and DevOps principles.

Contribute to projects spanning data warehousing, analytics enablement, and AI-ready data platforms.

Guide and mentor junior consultants, sharing best practices in engineering, design, and delivery.

Participate in solution design workshops and contribute to technical strategy discussions with stakeholders.

Help clients avoid building the wrong thing faster — focusing on practical, valuable data solutions that stand the test of time.




What Sets Candidates Apart

Technical capability is expected. What stands out is passion. I want to see:

Side projects and active experimentation with AI

Genuine curiosity around emerging tech and a track record of self-directed learning

Evidence you're constantly evolving — a side hustle, a blog, a community contribution, anything that shows you care about the craft




Whats On Offer

Competitive salary package

Varied, challenging client projects across industries

Collaborative, low-ego culture where curiosity is genuinely valued

Clear growth pathways as the practice continues to scale

Hybrid Work out of Martin Place office in Sydney CBD (sorry, no relocation support provided, and must have full Australian work rights)

Work with some of the best people in the industry at the top of the game




Next Steps

If you’re a curious data engineer who enjoys solving real problems, working with clients, and designing well-architected data systems, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply with your CV and Siena will be in touch for a confidential discussion.




Sestra.Code

Beyond this role, we’ve also built Sestra.Code, a community supporting Mid to Executive Level Women in Technology, focused on sharing knowledge, opportunities, and support.

If you’re interested but not sure this role is the perfect fit, we still encourage you to apply so we can connect and discuss other opportunities within our network.

Job Title: JDE Solution Architect and Functional Lead
Ad Text:
What's the role?

We’re looking for a JD Edwards (JDE) Solution Architect & Functional Lead to take ownership of the design, optimisation, and long-term strategy of our JDE EnterpriseOne environment. This role combines enterprise architecture, functional leadership, and technical oversight to ensure our ERP ecosystem supports business growth, operational efficiency, and continuous innovation.

Working closely with both business and technology stakeholders, you’ll act as a trusted advisor across the organisation - translating complex business requirements into scalable, integrated solutions while driving best practice across architecture, governance, and delivery. You’ll play a key role in shaping the ERP roadmap, improving core business processes, and leading the adoption of modern JDE capabilities and integration strategies.

Ideal for someone who enjoys balancing strategic thinking with hands-on functional expertise, this is an opportunity to influence enterprise-wide outcomes within a complex and evolving environment.

Responsibilities

Lead the end-to-end solution architecture and design of JD Edwards EnterpriseOne implementations, enhancements, and integrations, ensuring alignment with enterprise security, infrastructure, and business standards.

Act as the functional lead across core JDE modules including Financials, Procurement, and Project Costing, driving process optimisation and system improvement initiatives.

Provide technical oversight across all JDE development and integration activities, ensuring best practice in coding standards, system performance, and cross-platform connectivity.

Partner with senior business and technology stakeholders to provide strategic guidance on ERP capabilities, solution sizing, cost assessments, and long-term roadmap planning.

Establish and maintain governance frameworks, design review processes, and documentation standards to support scalable and sustainable enterprise solutions.

Drive continuous improvement and innovation through the adoption of modern JDE capabilities, including Orchestrator and UX One, while remaining aligned to Oracle’s roadmap and industry best practice.

Who are you?

8–12+ years of hands-on experience with JD Edwards EnterpriseOne, holding a Bachelor’s degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field.

At least 5 years’ experience in a Solution Architect or Functional Leadership role, with strong exposure to complex enterprise environments and JDE technical architecture.

Strong technical capability across JDE E1 development tools including C Business Functions, Event Rules (ER), RDA, Orchestrator, SQL, and API integrations, with experience across large-scale, multi-platform integration landscapes.

Proven experience leading full lifecycle ERP implementations and upgrades, including ongoing alignment to ERP roadmaps and Oracle release cycles.

Ability to operate across both business and technical stakeholders, translating complex requirements into clear, scalable solutions while influencing executive decision making and cross-functional outcomes.

Experience working in environments that span solution design, production stability, governance, and continuous improvement, balancing structure with practical delivery.

Strong analytical and problem-solving capability, with the ability to lead through influence, manage competing priorities, and deliver in fast-paced, complex environments.

Why News Corp Australia

Join a culture where innovation is our operating rhythm. You’ll work on a diverse portfolio of Australia’s most loved brands and play a pivotal role in leading the technological shift in how we create commercial content. Our vibrant, hybrid work environment and dynamic, values-driven culture empowers you to push boundaries, collaborate as one unified team, and make a positive, measurable impact for all Australians.

News Extras Benefits is a commitment to our people for their valued contribution to News, our audiences, clients and communities.

Our benefits include:

Exclusively ours - Access to publications and products from across the News Corp family

Perks & discounts - Exclusive offers across a range of products and services that deliver genuine cost savings and benefits

Health & wellbeing - Advice, support and facilities for healthy minds and bodies (including an onsite gym at our Adelaide, Brisbane & Sydney offices)

Time off - News offers a range of leave options to support your personal needs and moments that matter in life: Parental leave, Birthday leave, Volunteer leave, Career break and Compassionate leave.

Financial wellbeing - Support on making the most of your money

In addition to our perks and benefits, employees have access to a number of initiatives and programs that support their wellbeing and professional growth, such as:

Helping you grow - Access tailored development opportunities, including leadership programs, to cultivate your skills and support your career

Giving back - Make a difference to our communities through volunteer opportunities and company-organised charity drives

Workplace Inclusion and facilities – Engage with your passions through various employee-resource groups and accessibility to multi-faith quiet rooms & parents’ rooms

Who are we?

News Corp Australia is the nation’s most influential media organisation, operating more than 150 household-name brands across print, digital and multimedia. We lead the way in travel, fashion, food, health, homes and lifestyle, and we are the largest publisher across print and digital, reaching more than 16 million Australians each month.

What’s next?

Apply now or for more information on careers at News Corp Australia visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Diversity strengthens our national teams and newsroom as we seek to tell stories that represent the communities of Australia. Our people make us unique, and so we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, religion, sexual orientation, age, disability or gender identity. Applicants of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply!

We are committed to ensuring an accessible and supportive recruitment process. If you require any adjustments or accommodations at any stage, please do not hesitate to let us know here. In order for us to assist you, please ensure you include the role title you are applying for in the subject line. For more information regarding Diversity at NewsCorp Australia please visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers under the Diversity@News section.

Valuation Actuary


12 Month Contract

This is an opportunity for an experienced actuarial professional to support the Head of Actuarial Valuation and play a pivotal role in shaping Greenstone’s commercial financial strategy. As Valuation Actuary you will be central to delivering high-quality actuarial analysis and insights that inform strategic decision-making across our organisation.

The successful person will be responsible for maintaining and enhancing Greenstone’s embedded value models, an essential component of Greenstone's broader data-driven strategy. Working closely with leaders across the business, this role will contribute to company-wide initiatives, product development, and service offerings.


What are we looking for? 

Tertiary qualifications in mathematics or a related technical discipline, combined with an actuarial qualification (AIAA or higher)
3+ years’ relevant actuarial or analytics experience (life insurance experience advantageous but not essential)  
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills with a conceptual understanding of a range of statistical and machine learning modelling techniques
Ability to clearly communicate complex actuarial concepts to non-technical stakeholders, supported by strong written and verbal communication skills
Experience managing large-scale data and model systems, with hands-on coding capability (SQL and Python preferred)
Strong understanding of the linkages between actuarial outputs and finance data and systems, ensuring consistency between valuation, forecasting, and financial reporting
Experience in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial, data-driven environment (such as consulting or a start-up) is highly valued




Our Promise to Our People

We are passionate about our core values of Customer First, We Care, One Team, and Results Driven, which define how we approach our work. We recognise that our colleagues are the key to our success. That's why you can expect to be rewarded for the hard work you put in through job satisfaction, career development and the fantastic range of benefits we offer:

Fantastic employee benefits including flexi days, flexible working, annual $1200 travel/parking allowance & more

State of the art building facilities including a dedicated & subsidised on-site company café (weekly free lunch) & on-site gym
Health & well-being initiatives and programs
Opportunity to take part in in-house leadership & emotional intelligence workshops
Quarterly award functions and a grand annual conference to recognise our people
Retail discounts e.g. movie tickets, live shows + more
Morsl market (a fully automated self-serve, food and drink marketplace with a wide variety of meals, snacks and drinks)


Who We Are

Greenstone distributes insurance products under trusted brands in Australia, New Zealand and Canada. Some of our brands are Buddy, Australian Seniors, New Zealand Seniors, OneChoice, Real Insurance, SPCA Pet Insurance, North Cover, FiftyUp, Guardian and Choosi.

We are the leading distributor of direct Insurance products in the Australian insurance market. Since launching into the Financial Services sector in 2007, Greenstone has experienced a commanding volume of growth being recognised for our credible brands, customer focused values and people-driven work culture.

We are passionate about our core values, which contribute towards the positive culture that supports our employees, our customers and our community.

Location

Great central location in Norwest Business Park, Norwest, only a short walk from Norwest Metro station and also direct bus access from Parramatta and Blacktown.

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst, Risk and Operations
Ad Text:
Build your career and Australia’s future. 

Not many people can say they are working on building Australia’s future. With us you’ll be doing just that, leaving a legacy for all Australians. Plus, there’s equal employment, great training, and true flexible working arrangements.   

We have an exciting opportunity at nbn for a Senior Business Compliance Analyst, supporting our Field Services organisation and reporting into the Executive Manager Engagement and Business Management. 




A bit about your role 

An opportunity is available for a Senior Business Analyst (Risk & Operations) to support Field Services through the delivery of robust risk management practices and strong business management support. 

This role is primarily focused on key accountabilities that represent the largest components of the position: Risk Management and Business Management (BM) initiatives. You will support the Operations and Corporate Risk teams to deliver first line risk activities, including maintaining the Field Services risk register (ServiceNow), coordinating quarterly risk reviews, tracking treatment plans, and providing timely input into monthly and quarterly risk reporting. 

In addition, you will deliver a range of BM analysis and initiatives across Field Services, including ad hoc project support, supporting and guiding procurement processes and responding to procurement-related queries, supporting senior leaders, delivering attrition analysis and reporting, conducting exit interviews, and managing applications and submissions for industry awards. 

You will also contribute to Data Privacy, Security and Regulatory Compliance, including supporting privacy and security matters and providing analysis and insights to assist with security and fraud investigations, with findings and outcomes submitted to the Field Services SLT as required. Safety and wellbeing underpins all aspects of the role. 

 

A bit about you 

You are an experienced analyst with demonstrated capability in a risk, data privacy, or data security compliance role within a complex operational environment. You have a proven ability to implement and maintain governance and reporting disciplines, and to translate frameworks and policy requirements into practical, fit-for-purpose ways of working. 

 

You will bring: 

Demonstrated experience supporting first line risk management activities, including risk registers, risk governance, reporting, treatment plan tracking, and risk reviews. 

Experience supporting assurance activities, including control testing and engagement with internal audit/second line teams. 

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to develop productive relationships and provide clear, professional advice across operational and corporate stakeholders. 

Capability to deliver business management initiatives, including project support, procurement process support, workforce and attrition reporting, and operational governance activities. 

Advanced analytical skills, including advanced Excel and Power BI, with strong attention to detail, planning and prioritisation. 

 

Life at nbn 

To be part of nbn is to be part of something bigger. There’s so much more from here. Our pioneering spirit drives us forward every day. Together, we help lift the digital capability of the nation by building and connecting Australia’s best fibre infrastructure into homes and businesses, plus places and things, everywhere. We’re continuing to create Australia’s network—a network made for more. Not just a way for everyone to do the things they love but transforming the way they do them. 

 

A more inclusive working world 

nbn is committed to an inclusive, flexible, and supportive workplace where everyone can feel heard, valued, and respected. We believe diverse perspectives are the key to unlocking greater outcomes and encourage applications from people of all ages, nationalities, abilities, and cultures. That's why we have a Reconciliation Action Plan, Accessibility & Inclusion Plan, offer 18 weeks of paid parental leave, are a Pride in Diversity Platinum Employer, and have active employee-led diversity pillars. Click here to see our list of employee benefits and why we're recognised as a WORK180 Endorsed Employer for Women. 




Equal Opportunities for All 

We champion equal opportunities for all employees. If you have any accessibility requirements and would like to discuss adjustments for the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to contact us by emailing recruitment@nbnco.com.au or calling our Recruitment Accessibility Enquiry Line at +61 • •••• •990. Please note, this line is dedicated to accessibility-related enquiries for the purposes of recruitment, and only enquiries related to accessibility adjustments will be answered. For other matters, please visit our Contact Us page on our website. 




Where to from here? 

If you think this role might be for you, we want to hear from you. Please apply by following the links and sending in your resume only. We do not require a cover letter or any selection criteria to be addressed. 

To be eligible for this role, you must have full working rights in Australia.

Sydney, Newcastle, Canberra, Adelaide, Perth, Brisbane OR Melbourne CBD location/ hybrid work from home
Work collaboratively with internal and external stakeholders
Permanent full-Time role

Purpose of the Role:

Suncorp's Partner Performance team manages over 60 contracted personal injury providers (including rehabilitation, medico-legal, imaging, exercise physiology, psychology and specialist providers), servicing eight CTP and Workers Compensation schemes nationally. The team works with the Claims teams to optimise provider performance and deliver the best possible outcomes for injured people in the various schemes. These providers operate within a highly regulated environment and are managed within Suncorp's procurement, risk and provider management frameworks. Governance activities are required to manage these contracts in accordance with these frameworks.

The core task is ongoing management of the cohort of contracted providers assigned to the role, aligned to the scope of the Partner Performance team. This includes end-to-end responsibility for contract management of the providers assigned to the role, which are subject to change in order to meet evolving business needs. The role requires strong governance, engagement and relationship management skills with an attention to detail and ability to analyse and document performance data. Key stakeholders are the eight personal injury schemes, internal functions (including Risk, Compliance and Security Assurance) and external contracted providers. Familiarity with personal injury claims management and/or a background in allied health is an advantage.

Key Accountabilities (b u t not limited to):

Ongoing engagement and stakeholder management with internal and external stakeholders to address issues and improve performance
Conducting regular performance meetings with contracted providers including the preparation of meeting packs, documentation of activities and the promotion of productive collaboration with providers
Management of contract establishment, changes and renewals in collaboration with Procurement and Risk
Completion of risk artifacts through Suncorp's Ariba Third Party Risk Management (TPRM) platform
Management of provider adherence to EPSS (data security) requirements.


What you'll bring:

Minimum 3 years appropriate industry experience in insurance, financial services, provider management or allied health
Experience in stakeholder and contract management an advantage
Experience in governance, risk, compliance or quality roles an advantage
Experience with personal injury claims management and use of providers an advantage
Ability to be proactive and work autonomously within a diverse working environment.


What You'll Enjoy:

Access our award-winning Shine platform - your central hub for exclusive perks, wellbeing support, career development, and recognition. Shine has been recognised for excellence in employee experience, making it easier than ever to access what matters most to you.

Hybrid working, flexible leave, 20 weeks paid parental leave, and up to 5 days paid flexible leave annually
Paid study support, career breaks, on-the-job learning, and a $1,000 referral bonus
Free wellbeing coaching, a dedicated Employee Council, and confidential EAP access
Discounts at 400+ retailers, insurance savings, gym memberships, and reduced super fees
Paid volunteer leave, donation matching, and unlimited emergency response leave
Performance-based bonuses and Suncorp shares for eligible employees
Diversity is celebrated through employee resource groups and inclusive policies for all families.

Explore our full benefits: www.suncorpgroup.com.au/careers/benefits

Join FNZ’s Capital Markets Product Management team as a Business Analyst and play a key role in shaping solutions on our Syn~ platform - a market-leading post-trade capability supporting middle and back-office processing across multiple markets and asset classes. You will work closely with customers and internal stakeholders to understand desired business outcomes, translate these into clear functional and technical requirements, and ensure the right solutions are delivered into production.




In this role, you will be responsible for business and functional solution design, technical analysis of Syn~ platform capabilities, business process analysis, and end-to-end testing (including supporting client UAT). We are looking for someone who is solution and execution focused, comfortable solving complex problems in a securities processing context, and able to take ownership for outcomes on behalf of customers while building strong relationships across a wide stakeholder group.




The responsibilities will include:  

Gather and analyse business requirements through consultation with broking, clearing and custody clients, as well as exchanges, clearing houses and regulators.

Produce high-quality requirements documentation (L2–L3), including user stories, scenarios, user journeys and solution designs, and support solution estimation activities.

Develop end-to-end test scenarios and contribute to regression, industry and sanity test packs to support robust product delivery and quality outcomes.

Build and maintain a strong understanding of Syn~ platform functionality, underlying business processes and capital markets workflows.

Partner closely with Product Owners and Solution Consultants to design solutions aligned to agreed scope, ensuring both business and technical requirements are met.

Engage with customers through workshops and discussions, providing consultative input to shape requirements and solutions.

Support platform implementation activities, including definition of data loads, migration scripts and migration procedures.

Deliver product demonstrations and training to both internal stakeholders and clients, enabling effective adoption of platform functionality.

Produce clear release notes and documentation (including FNZ Assist content) to support product enhancements and ongoing user enablement.

Support defect resolution activities, including investigation and root cause analysis, and ensure non-functional requirements are captured and documented.







What are we ideally looking for in a successful candidate:

Proven experience in business analysis, ideally within a financial services or securities back-office environment.

Strong understanding of post-trade workflows, with experience across banking, brokerage or custody operations; experience within an Australian bank or broker is highly regarded.

Knowledge of ASX Clear and ASX Settlement (CHESS) processes and messaging, with additional exposure to SWIFT or other clearing house messaging considered advantageous.

Experience working with capital markets platforms, with exposure to FNZ Syn~ or comparable systems preferred.

Demonstrated ability to operate in a solution-oriented, delivery-focused environment, taking ownership and driving outcomes.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret complex data and translate this into practical solutions.

Commercially aware, with the ability to understand and balance client, product and delivery considerations.

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with confidence engaging both internal and external clients.

Highly organised, with strong time management and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

A proactive, self-directed approach to work, with a focus on continuous improvement and capability development.

About the job




Investment Associate – Tin Alley Ventures Pty Ltd

Location: Carlton, Melbourne, Australia

Commitment: Full-time, fixed term

Reports to: Managing Partner

 

About the job

Tin Alley Ventures Pty Ltd is hiring an Investment Associate to help us identify, diligence, and support deep-tech investments and company creation emerging from the University of Melbourne and partner institutes.

This is a high-trust, high-pace role for someone who can move fluidly between frontier science and commercial judgement, someone who thrives on learning quickly, writing clearly, and making strong decisions with imperfect information.




What You’ll Do

Identify and assess high-potential research and early ventures from academics, research labs, spinout pipelines, and the broader deep-tech ecosystem.

Translate complex technical material into investable insight; mapping the core “truth,” the key risks, and the milestones needed to unlock value.

Run commercial diligence: market mapping, customer and partner conversations, competitive analysis, adoption barriers, pricing/value hypotheses, and go-to-market pathways.

Support technical diligence: literature reviews, expert calls, IP/claims framing, technical roadmaps, and milestone planning (with internal and external experts).

Build crisp investment materials: screening notes, internal briefs, investment memos, and (where relevant) models and scenario analyses.

Work alongside Tin Alley Ventures’ team, syndicate partners, and university stakeholders to progress opportunities from “interesting” to “investable.”

Support founders and portfolio companies with targeted projects (fundraising materials, hiring support, strategic analysis, introductions, and operational problem-solving).

Contribute to a high-performance culture: act with integrity, curiosity, and urgency — and make the team better.




What We’re Looking For

Tertiary qualifications. A top-tier academic record, with strong scientific depth (a MD or PhD in a scientific discipline is preferred).

2–3 years’ experience in a top-tier environment such as MBB (McKinsey/Bain/BCG) or bulge-bracket investment banking (GS-equivalent) or a similarly demanding, high-standard role.

Exceptional logical thinking, writing and synthesis; you can make the complicated simple without making it wrong.

Strong analytical horsepower paired with practical judgement (you know what matters, what doesn’t, and what to do next).

Ability to build trust quickly with researchers, founders, and investors and to ask sharp questions without ego.

Comfort with ambiguity, rapid learning, and high autonomy; you operate with urgency and care.

Genuine alignment with Tin Alley Ventures’ values: Daring, Excellence, Curiosity, Integrity, Partnership, Fun.




Non-traditional backgrounds are explicitly welcome. If you’re “quirky but exceptional”, top sportsperson with elite discipline, a brilliant lab scientist ready to build, a founder/operator, or someone with an unconventional path who has done something undeniably outstanding; we want to hear from you.




Nice to Have

Exposure to venture capital (as an investor, founder raising capital, or working in an incubator/accelerator).

Experience in deep tech areas such as biotech, advanced materials, AI/ML, robotics, energy, or climate tech.

Strong financial modelling, scenario analysis, and capital planning skills.

Evidence you’ve built something real: a product, a company, a program, a research platform, a community — anything with traction and proof.




What You’ll Get

A front-row seat in one of Australia’s most interesting deep-tech ecosystems

High ownership and steep learning across sourcing, diligence, decision-making, and founder support.

Direct access to a network of founders, researchers, operators, co-investors, and domain experts.

Mentorship and apprenticeship in deep-tech venture investing and company creation.

A real potential pathway to Founder-in-Residence at the end of the year, if mutually agreed.




Salary: Competitive




Visa: sponsorship available
